content: I'm so glad it's finally Friday. I've been looking forward to this weekend all week. I'm planning on working on my documentary about chess.
That sounds like a fascinating project. What inspired you to create a documentary about chess?
I've been playing chess for years, and I've always been fascinated by the strategy and psychology behind the game. I've been filming some of the local chess tournaments in Lisbon.
Lisbon has a rich cultural scene. Have you featured any of the local chess players in your documentary?
Yes, I've been following the story of a young player named Carlos. He's been competing in some of the international tournaments and has a really inspiring story.
That sounds like a great story to tell. Are you planning on submitting your documentary to any film festivals?
I'm actually planning on submitting it to the Lisbon Film Festival in November. I'm a bit nervous, but I think it's a great opportunity to get my work seen.
The Lisbon Film Festival is a great platform. What else do you like to do in your free time, aside from working on your documentary?
I'm also really into Brazilian jiu-jitsu. I've been training for a few years now, and I love the physical and mental challenge of it.
Brazilian jiu-jitsu is a great way to stay active and focused. Do you have a favorite training partner or instructor?
My instructor, Paulo, is great. He's been teaching me for years, and he's always pushing me to improve my technique.
